skill.name: youtube-transcript-analysis-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube video transcripts and perform deep competitive analysis. Analyze content strategy, value propositions, target audiences, pain points, and content gaps from any YouTube video.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube transcripts and perform 8-dimension competitive content analysis.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: This skill extracts YouTube video transcripts using the BrowserAct API and performs structured 8-dimension content analysis. The static scanner flagged network access to a third-party API and environment variable access for API credentials, but these are legitimate use patterns. API key is read from environment variables using os.getenv(), not hardcoded. Network requests are limited to BrowserAct API endpoints only. No command injection vulnerabilities exist - script arguments are passed as Python arguments, not shell commands.

medium_findings.title: Script Execution with External API Call
medium_findings.description: The skill executes Python scripts that make network requests to BrowserAct API. This is legitimate API-based functionality but creates elevated trust boundary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network requests are to a documented third-party API service. The skill requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment, which is proper credential management. No evidence of data exfiltration or malicious network behavior.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Credentials
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables at line 204. This is secure credential management - no hardcoded secrets found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: API key is retrieved via os.getenv() and never hardcoded. This is the recommended secure approach for credential management.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Third-Party API Endpoints
low_findings.description: API base URL and error page URLs are hardcoded to BrowserAct service endpoints. These are legitimate service endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded URLs point to the legitimate BrowserAct API service (api.browseract.com). No dynamic URL construction from user input observed.

content.user_title: Extract and Analyze YouTube Transcripts for Competitive Intelligence
content.value_statement: Manually watching competitor YouTube videos to extract insights is time-consuming and inconsistent. This skill automates transcript extraction and provides structured 8-dimension analysis for content strategy, audience research, and competitive positioning.
actual_capabilities: Extract transcripts from single YouTube videos using BrowserAct API
Batch extract transcripts from multiple videos by keyword search
Perform 8-dimension structured analysis on transcript content
Identify target audience profiles and viewer intent from video content
Map competitor pain points, value propositions, and CTA strategies
Generate content gap analysis with strategic opportunities
limitations: Requires BrowserAct API key for transcript extraction
Cannot bypass geo-restricted or private YouTube videos
Analysis quality depends on transcript availability and accuracy

use_cases.title: Competitive Content Strategy Research
use_cases.description: Extract and analyze transcripts from competitor YouTube videos to understand their messaging, positioning, and content gaps. Use insights to differentiate your own content strategy.

use_cases.title: Audience Research and Persona Development
use_cases.description: Analyze multiple videos in a niche to identify recurring pain points, terminology, and audience signals. Build detailed buyer personas from actual content.

use_cases.title: Content Gap Discovery
use_cases.description: Batch analyze top-performing videos in your industry to find topics and angles that competitors have not covered well. Generate content ideas with validated demand.

prompt_templates.title: Single Video Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTube Transcript Analysis skill to analyze this video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides a specific competitor video URL for analysis

prompt_templates.title: Batch Competitive Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTube Transcript Analysis skill to batch analyze the top 5 videos about 'KEYWORD' from this week.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to research multiple videos on a topic

prompt_templates.title: Content Gap Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTube Transcript Analysis skill to analyze videos from competitors [channel1, channel2] about 'TOPIC'. Focus on identifying content gaps.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to find underserved topics in competitor content

prompt_templates.title: CTA Strategy Benchmarking
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTube Transcript Analysis skill to analyze [URL1, URL2, URL3] focusing on business model and conversion strategies.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to understand how competitors drive conversions

output_examples.output:
Video Metadata
- Title: How to Build a SaaS in 30 Days
- Channel: Tech Creator
- Views: 245K

8-Dimension Analysis
1. Hook: Opens with bold claim 'I built this in one month' - curiosity gap
2. Core Message: Anyone can build SaaS without coding experience
3. Audience: Early-stage founders, hobbyist builders
4. Pain Points: Technical barriers, overwhelm, fear of failure
5. Viral Drivers: Relatable struggle, actionable framework, success story
6. Credibility: Showcases actual product, shares metrics
7. Business Model: Email list building, course upsell
8. Content Gaps: No pricing discussion, lacks technical deep-dive

output_examples.input: Batch analyze top 3 videos about 'AI tools for productivity'
output_examples.output:
Cross-Analysis Summary
- Common Value Props: Time savings, workflow automation, beginner-friendly
- Target Audience: Knowledge workers, productivity enthusiasts, solopreneurs
- Recurring Pain Points: Information overload, tool fatigue, lack of systems
- Dominant Strategy: Problem-agitate-solve narrative, tool comparisons
- Differentiation: Some focus on specific niches (writers vs marketers)
- Industry Gaps: No content on team collaboration with AI tools

best_practices: Provide specific video URLs for precise analysis rather than broad keyword searches
Use batch analysis to compare multiple competitors before developing content strategy
Focus on the Analysis Focus parameter to concentrate on relevant dimensions
anti_patterns: Do not request transcripts from videos with no subtitles - analysis will be incomplete
Avoid analyzing auto-generated transcripts without noting quality issues in analysis
Do not output raw transcript text to users - it is too long and not useful

faq.question: What API key do I need for this skill?
faq.answer: This skill requires a BrowserAct API key. Get one free at browseract.com/reception/integrations.

faq.question: Can I analyze private or geo-restricted videos?
faq.answer: No. This skill extracts publicly available transcripts. Private, age-restricted, or geo-blocked videos may not work.

faq.question: How many videos can I analyze at once?
faq.answer: Batch mode supports up to 10 videos per run. Concurrent task limits depend on your BrowserAct plan.

faq.question: What languages are supported for analysis?
faq.answer: The skill supports transcript extraction in all languages that YouTube provides auto-generated or manual subtitles. Analysis can be written in any language you specify.

faq.question: How accurate are the transcripts?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on YouTube subtitles. Auto-generated transcripts may have errors. The skill notes transcript quality issues in the analysis.

faq.question: What does the 8-dimension analysis cover?
faq.answer: Content structure and hook, core messaging, audience persona and intent, pain points and emotional arc, viral drivers, evidence and credibility, business model and conversion, and content gaps.
